DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANTS 45 LANSING DEVELOPMENT, LLC and BUILD GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED-CROSS COMPLAINT as to the third and sixth causes of action in the second amended cross-complaint filed by Lambert Development Lansing, LLC, Lambert Development, LLC, Cannon Constructors North, Inc. and Cannon Constructors, Inc. is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. Cross-complainants adequately allege that the contracts sued on are written contracts. (SACC 30 and 48.) The failure to attach the written contracts to the SACC does not require sustaining the demurrer. Per Miles v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Company (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 394, 401-402, a party may plead the legal effect of a contract and need not attach the contract or plead its terms verbatim. The court did not consider the argument raised by cross-defendants for the first time in their reply brief that the third and sixth causes of action are inadequately pled for reasons other than the failure to state whether the contracts are written, oral, or implied and the failure to attach the contracts. Cross-complainants were not given the opportunity to respond to this argument. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Text Ruling

CROSS-DEFENDANTS 45 LANSING DEVELOPMENT, LLC and BUILD GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED in its entirety. The motion to strike paragraphs 30-34 and 48-52 (items 3-7 and 10-14 in the notice) is denied for the same reasons set forth in the ruling on cross-defendants' demurrer. The motion to strike paragraph 29 and any reference to "designs" in paragraphs 36 and 37 (items 1-2 and 8-9 in the notice) is denied because 45 Lansing Development and Build Group are not the purported professional engineer which a CCP 411.35 certificate of merit was meant to protect. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Appearances: Suzanne Norris of Law Offices of John A. Hauser telephone number 714-371-2730 appeared by telephone for Cross-Defendant Roy E. Hanson Jr. Manufacturing.

Ms. Norris stated that she has nothing to add. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Cross-defendants 45 Lansing Development, LLC and Build Group, Inc.'s motion regarding objection to Pre-trial Order No. 3 is denied. The Special Master properly determined that there was good cause to permit cross-complainants Lambert Development Lansing LLC, Lambert Development LLC, Cannon Constructors North, Inc. and Cannon Constructors, Inc. (collectively Lambert) to serve the document requests that are the subject of Pre-Trial Order No. 3. The document requests meet the standard of discovery relevance since they are all related to Lambert's cross-claims and the objectors have not shown that the document requests are unduly burdensome. The fact that the objectors believe that Lambert's cross-claims relating to the 45 Lansing property lack merit is not a basis to preclude Lambert from pursuing discovery directed at those claims. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Off Calendar May-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Giampaoli of Collinsworth, Specht, Calkins & Giampaoli, LLP telephone number 925-400-9575 for Cross-Complainants Lambert Development Lansing LLC, Lambert Development LLC, Cannon Constructors North Inc. and Cannon Constructors.
Peter Dye of Koeller, Nebeker, Carlson & Haluck LLP telephone number 916-724-5700 for Cross-Defendant 45 Lansing Development, LLC.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT 45 LANSING DEVELOPMENT LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is overruled. A third-party beneficiary of a contract need not be named or identified individually to be an express beneficiary. Spinks, 141 CA4th at 1022-23. Here, issues of fact and intent are not susceptible to determination at the demurrer stage. Order signed in open Court.
